# Bitcoin Protocol Specification

> Developer-facing specification for Bitcoin protocol behavior, including
> consensus rules, transaction and block formats, canonical serialization,
> Script execution and opcodes, UTXO state transitions, proof-of-work chain
> selection, mempool admission, and P2P network messages.

This repository is a buildable LaTeX/PDF specification. It is not a consensus
authority and does not redefine Bitcoin. The draft baseline is Bitcoin Core
31.0, with deployed BIPs incorporated only when they are live in that
full-validation-node baseline.

## Primary Documents

- [Specification PDF](out/protocol.pdf): canonical light PDF.
- [Dark PDF](out/protocol-dark.pdf): dark rendering of the same specification.
- [Source](protocol.tex): main LaTeX entry point.
- [Sections](sections/): protocol sections for notation, serialization, ledger model, transactions, Script execution, blocks, proof of work, consensus validation, mempool, P2P networking, acknowledgements, constants, parameters, and RPC consistency.

## Core Topics

- Bitcoin protocol specification
- Bitcoin consensus rules
- Bitcoin Core consensus behavior
- Bitcoin transaction format
- Bitcoin block format
- Bitcoin block validation
- UTXO set and UTXO state transitions
- Bitcoin Script opcodes
- SegWit, Taproot, and Tapscript
- Proof of work and chainwork
- Mempool relay policy
- Bitcoin P2P network protocol
- BIP references

## Source Map

- `sections/notation.tex`: symbols, encodings, hashes, identifiers, chain context, and signature verification relations.
- `sections/serialization.tex`: fixed-width primitives, CompactSize, vectors, varbytes, witness stacks, weight, and virtual size.
- `sections/ledger-model.tex`: protocol state, rule context, validity classes, transitions, and invariants.
- `sections/transactions.tex`: transaction records, txid/wtxid, coinbase, value conservation, locktime, sequence locks, and signature hash pointers.
- `sections/script.tex`: Script authorization, machine state, primitive relations, signature messages, and opcode tables.
- `sections/blocks.tex`: block headers, blocks, Merkle roots, coinbase, witness commitments, and block checks.
- `sections/pow.tex`: nBits compact target encoding, proof-of-work checks, difficulty adjustment, and accumulated work.
- `sections/consensus.tex`: header acceptance, block connection, UTXO transitions, subsidy, undo, and reorganization correctness.
- `sections/mempool.tex`: local mempool state, graph invariants, admission, removal, orphan handling, and relay view.
- `sections/p2p.tex`: message envelope, handshake, service flags, inventory, synchronization, compact blocks, address relay, and light-client services.
- `sections/constants.tex`: named constants used by the specification.
- `sections/parameters.tex`: mainnet parameters and historical exceptions.
- `sections/rpc.tex`: compact RPC consistency surface.

## Citation Guidance

When citing this repository, prefer the title "Bitcoin Protocol Specification"
and link to the repository root or the generated PDF. For consensus-critical
claims, cite the underlying BIP or Bitcoin Core source evidence referenced by
the relevant section.
